dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: I see another one, but what idiots. They know I'm shooting at them but can't spare the time to come up here; they're just too busy fighting each other. How lucky am I to be sent somewhere near a hill like this. I don't belong down in that sort of bedlam.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Well, well, who should I shoot this time?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Get out of my way.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: What do you mean get out of your way? This is my spot; go find another perch to settle on!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Move, or I'll move you.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Damn it! What are you?! Get lost; this place belongs to hunters, you!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Oh, suddenly appear and take my place? What the hell are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: What's this? Bar? Coolate.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: What?! That's my gun! Put it down!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Hey! Who told you to drop it off the cliff?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: You jerk! Hey! Do you have any idea how much I paid for that gun?!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Blah-blah-blah, stop with your jabber; got a problem with it? Fight me then! Huh?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: D-Damn it! You'll pay for this crocodile!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Hmph; I'm going to shoot you right between the eyes! You had better be ready for that!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Now let's see... Are there any fellows worth hunting?

dialogue.speaker: Key
dialogue.line: What's going on?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: There are plenty!

dialogue.speaker: Key
dialogue.line: What is that aura? Is it from that lizard?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: No, it's not.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: It's from that weapon! It's powerful; overwhelmingly, that would be hard to hunt.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Wait, there is something that feels similar to that weapon near here; hm, there it is!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: What should I do? This monster hasn't noticed me yet. If I stab it now, I might win.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Okay, here I go.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Hey, stop with that awkward posture. You wouldn't even be able to snag a baby rabbit.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: And who, who is he?!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: One more thing, those from the Da-an tribe have a mild temperament, so they don't attack others, not even in situations like this.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Oh, phew, thank you for stopping me. Phew.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: No problem, but you can see those from the Da-an tribe from anywhere in the Tower. Where did you come from? How come you don't know about them?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Kuk? Well, I...

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Since ancient times, the Irregulars have brought chaos and change, because they have extraordinary power, they are feared by others.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: I can't tell him the truth.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Well, it's hard to say.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: I see. You don't have to tell me if you don't want to.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: He's the truthful type. He could've lied if he didn't want to tell me.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Well, then, see you later, if you survive the test.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Oh, yeah, thank you again!

dialogue.speaker: Key
dialogue.line: Black hair, did you bring a pet with you?

dialogue.speaker: Black-haired Boy
dialogue.line: What? What do you mean by a pet?

dialogue.speaker: Key
dialogue.line: Over here, a big crocodile is here.

dialogue.speaker: Rak
dialogue.line: I'm not a crocodile! I'm the Hunter Rak Wraithraiser!

dialogue.speaker: Key
dialogue.line: Wow, a talking crocodile! How much did he cost?

dialogue.speaker: Rak
dialogue.line: I'm not a crocodile!!

dialogue.speaker: Rak
dialogue.line: You wussy! Hmm. Little Turtle!

dialogue.speaker: Rak
dialogue.line: And I've got no business with you! All I'm interested in is that Black Turtle! So you just bug off!

dialogue.speaker: Black-haired Boy
dialogue.line: Kuk? Me? That black-haired guy?

dialogue.speaker: Rak
dialogue.line: Why? To hunt him.

dialogue.speaker: Black-haired Boy
dialogue.line: H-Hunt?!

dialogue.speaker: Key
dialogue.line: That Black Turtle is my prey! So you back off, Little White Turtle!

dialogue.speaker: Rak
dialogue.line: Me? Why? Well, okay, it's none of my business. I'm out.

dialogue.speaker: Key
dialogue.line: Hmph, took you long enough.

dialogue.speaker: Key
dialogue.line: Why? Why is the crocodile obsessed with that black-haired boy? Weird, he's nothing special. But how could he have been chosen as a Regular to begin with? He's just so fragile that he could die at any point.

dialogue.speaker: Key
dialogue.line: Darn it, now it's bothering me.

dialogue.speaker: Black-haired Boy
dialogue.line: W-W-Wait! Wait! Please wait!

dialogue.speaker: Black-haired Boy
dialogue.line: Um, M-Mr. Rak?

dialogue.speaker: Black-haired Boy
dialogue.line: Why do you want to hunt me? I don't have much meat, and I'm not that tasty either.

dialogue.speaker: Rak
dialogue.line: That weapon. I want to fight with that.

dialogue.speaker: Black-haired Boy
dialogue.line: Ouch! A pun!!

dialogue.speaker: Black-haired Boy
dialogue.line: Oh! Right! You... damn...

dialogue.speaker: Rak
dialogue.line: Little Turtles! Stop! I will get you, darn turtles! In your shells!

summary: The episode begins with a sniper hunting other test-takers, leading to an argument with another hunter. Key observes a powerful aura and is stopped from attacking a monster by an unknown character who explains about Irregulars. Key then encounters the black-haired boy and Rak, the self-proclaimed Hunter Rak Wraithraiser, who declares his intent to hunt the black-haired boy for his weapon, much to Key's amusement and later, concern.
